quiz.title: SAT ENglish: Unit 2 Quiz 9
quiz.subtitle: Selecting Data That Logically Completes a Given Claim (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation

questions.question: Researchers studying sleep and memory found that participants who slept eight hours after learning a new skill showed 40% better retention than those who stayed awake. The data suggest that sleep enhances memory consolidation, as evidenced by ________.
options: comparable retention rates between groups who napped briefly versus those who remained awake
baseline memory assessments showing a 5-point advantage for the sleep-condition participants
a 40% retention advantage for the sleep group compared to the wake group
mood ratings improving by 12% in the sleep group alongside higher retention scores
questions.explanation: Choice C completes the claim by restating the key quantitative finding (40% advantage) that directly evidences consolidation. Choice A provides a precise comparison but tests an untested nap variable. Choice B offers a specific baseline metric that would actually weaken the causal claim. Choice D cites a quantified mood shift, a confounding variable not central to consolidation.

questions.question: A team analyzing urban air quality observed that neighborhoods with more tree canopy cover had lower average PM2.5 levels. The researchers concluded that vegetation filters particulate matter, a claim supported by ________.
options: property values increasing by 9% in neighborhoods following extensive tree planting initiatives
pedestrian traffic rising 25% in green spaces during seasons with full foliage coverage
a negative correlation between canopy percentage and PM2.5 concentration across sampled neighborhoods
consistent PM2.5 readings across neighborhoods regardless of the specific tree species planted
questions.explanation: Choice C logically completes the claim by specifying the quantitative relationship (negative correlation) that supports the filtering hypothesis. Choice A provides a precise economic metric irrelevant to air filtration. Choice B cites a quantified behavioral trend unrelated to particulate capture. Choice D offers a specific consistency claim that doesn't directly evidence the vegetation-PM2.5 link.

questions.question: Economists tracking remote work trends noted that cities with higher percentages of remote workers saw smaller declines in downtown retail revenue during off-peak hours. This pattern suggests that remote workers support local businesses, as shown by ________.
options: residential property values rising 14% in neighborhoods with high remote-work adoption rates
commercial lease rates declining 8% in central business districts following remote-work policies
consumer spending on home-office equipment increasing 22% in regions with flexible work
a positive relationship between remote-work prevalence and off-peak retail revenue stability
questions.explanation: Choice D completes the claim by identifying the specific quantitative pattern (positive relationship) that evidences remote workers' support for local businesses. Choice A provides a precise housing-market metric, not retail revenue. Choice B cites a quantified commercial real estate decline, inconsistent with the claim. Choice D focuses on equipment spending, not local business support.

questions.question: A study of exercise and cognitive function found that adults who engaged in moderate aerobic activity three times weekly showed improved executive function scores. The researchers attributed this to increased cerebral blood flow, a mechanism supported by ________.
options: self-reported energy levels increasing 18% among participants who exercised regularly
physical fitness metrics improving 31% compared to modest gains in cognitive test scores
similar cognitive gains in participants practicing yoga versus those completing aerobic routines
a 22% increase in prefrontal cortex perfusion measured via fMRI in the exercise group
questions.explanation: Choice D logically completes the claim by providing specific physiological data (22% perfusion increase) that evidences the proposed blood-flow mechanism. Choice A offers a quantified subjective report rather than objective cerebral metrics. Choice B compares outcome domains without isolating the causal pathway. Choice C introduces an untested exercise comparison that doesn't address blood flow.

questions.question: Linguists examining dialect contact observed that communities with higher rates of intergroup marriage showed faster convergence in vowel pronunciation. The researchers concluded that social integration drives linguistic change, as demonstrated by ________.
options: a strong positive correlation between intermarriage rates and vowel-convergence speed
lexical borrowing increasing 40% in communities with frequent cross-dialect media exposure
consonant stability remaining within 2% variance despite vowel-system changes across zones
younger speakers adopting merged vowels 30% faster than older speakers in all communities
questions.explanation: Choice A completes the claim by specifying the quantitative relationship (positive correlation) that evidences social integration as the driver of linguistic change. Choice B provides a precise lexical metric but introduces media exposure, a confounding variable. Choice C cites a specific stability range irrelevant to vowel-convergence. Choice D describes a quantified age effect that doesn't isolate social integration.

questions.question: Public health analysts found that regions implementing sugar-sweetened beverage taxes saw a 12% decline in pediatric obesity rates within three years. The analysts attributed this to reduced consumption of sugary drinks, a conclusion supported by ________.
options: a 19% reduction in sugary drink purchases among households with children in taxed regions
sales of untaxed beverages like bottled water increasing 14% in the same markets
obesity rates remaining stable within 1% in neighboring regions without beverage taxes
parental nutrition-label awareness rising 27% in regions with concurrent public health campaigns
questions.explanation: Choice A logically completes the claim by providing the specific consumption data (19% purchase reduction) that evidences the proposed mechanism. Choice B describes a quantified substitution effect that supports policy impact but doesn't directly evidence reduced sugary-drink consumption. Choice C provides a precise comparison-group metric, strengthening inference but not completing the mechanism claim. Choice D cites a quantified awareness shift, a confounding variable.

questions.question: Ecologists monitoring forest recovery after wildfire observed that plots with higher soil nitrogen levels showed faster regrowth of native plant species. The team concluded that nutrient availability limits post-fire succession, a hypothesis confirmed by ________.
options: species richness increasing 18% in plots receiving supplemental irrigation during dry seasons
a 3.2-fold increase in native seedling density per unit increase in soil nitrogen concentration
regrowth rates varying less than 5% across plots regardless of initial fire severity
microbial activity rising 24% in soils with elevated organic matter following the fire
questions.explanation: Choice B completes the claim by specifying the quantitative relationship (3.2-fold increase) that evidences nutrient limitation as the mechanism. Choice A provides a precise biodiversity metric but introduces water availability, a confounding variable. Choice C cites a specific variance range that doesn't isolate nutrient effects. Choice D addresses a quantified soil-health indicator distinct from nitrogen limitation.

questions.question: A cognitive science study found that participants who practiced retrieval (testing themselves) retained 50% more information after one week than those who restudied the same material. The researchers attributed this advantage to the 'testing effect,' as evidenced by ________.
options: confidence ratings improving 15% among participants using retrieval practice during learning
restudy groups spending 40% more time on task yet achieving lower delayed scores
immediate post-test performance matching within 3% before diverging on the delayed assessment
retrieval-practice participants demonstrating a 50% retention advantage over the restudy group
questions.explanation: Choice D logically completes the claim by restating the key quantitative finding (50% advantage) that evidences the testing effect. Choice A cites a quantified confidence shift, a subjective measure not central to retention. Choice B introduces effort as an alternative explanation, which would weaken the testing-effect claim. Choice C describes a precise timing pattern consistent with the effect but doesn't complete the claim with the specific evidentiary data.

questions.question: Urban planners analyzing traffic patterns observed that intersections with dedicated left-turn signals had 35% fewer rear-end collisions than those with permissive left turns. The planners concluded that signal specialization reduces conflict points, a claim supported by ________.
options: overall traffic throughput increasing 12% at signalized intersections compared to roundabouts
pedestrian satisfaction scores rising 18% in neighborhoods with upgraded traffic infrastructure
collision rates varying less than 4% across intersections regardless of time of day
a 35% reduction in rear-end collisions at intersections with dedicated left-turn signals
questions.explanation: Choice D completes the claim by specifying the quantitative outcome (35% reduction) that evidences the conflict-point reduction mechanism. Choice A provides a precise throughput metric, a different traffic outcome not central to collision reduction. Choice B cites a quantified satisfaction shift irrelevant to vehicle collision patterns. Choice C suggests timing invariance, which doesn't isolate signal design as the causal factor.

questions.question: Nutrition researchers found that participants who replaced refined grains with whole grains for eight weeks showed a 15% reduction in fasting insulin levels. The team attributed this to improved insulin sensitivity from increased fiber intake, a mechanism confirmed by ________.
options: weight loss averaging 6 pounds among participants reducing caloric intake regardless of grain type
insulin levels shifting 14% in participants increasing vegetable intake without altering grain choices
a 15% decline in fasting insulin alongside a 28% increase in daily fiber consumption
self-reported satiety scores rising 22% among those consuming whole grains versus refined grains
questions.explanation: Choice C logically completes the claim by linking the insulin reduction to the proposed fiber-increase mechanism with specific quantitative data. Choice A provides a precise weight metric but introduces caloric reduction, a confounding variable. Choice B cites a quantified insulin shift driven by vegetables, which would weaken the grain-specific claim. Choice D offers a quantified satiety metric, a subjective outcome not directly evidencing insulin sensitivity.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can accurately select data patterns that logically complete specific claims by evaluating mechanism alignment.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on whether the evidence directly addresses the exact mechanism or outcome stated in the claim.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: eliminate options that are true but irrelevant, or that support a different aspect of the argument.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Ask yourself: does this finding provide the specific quantitative evidence the claim requires to be complete?
